data.title: Trump Fed nominee Miran says he'll preserve central bank independence
data.description:
Stephen Miran, President Donald Trump's nominee for a Federal Reserve Board governor, said on Thursday he'll act independently based on his assessment of economic data and emphasized the importance of an independent central bank.

"The Federal Open Market Committee is an independent group with a monumental task, and I intend to preserve that independence and serve the American people to the best of my ability," he said in his confirmation hearing in the U.S. Senate.

"In my view, the most important job of the central bank is to prevent Depressions and hyperinflations," he said in his prepared opening remarks for the hearing. "Independence of monetary policy is a critical element for its success."

"It's important to hear a variety of views," he added in questioning by Senator Tim Scott (R-SC).

He sees the need for a full review of the Fed's regulatory function, he added. Miran also said he'll push back on the central bank straying from its mandate. For example, he considers the Fed's actions regarding climate change to be highly political.

The nominee declined to say whether he believed the Bureau of Labor Statistics' jobs data was "faked," but repeated several times that he believes the quality of various measures have steadily deteriorated.

He also refused to give a "yes" or "no" answer on whether Donald Trump lost the 2020 election. Instead, he responded that Congress certified the election of Joe Biden as president.

Miran said he'll take an unpaid leave from his current role heading the Council of Economic Advisers. He was nominated to fill the vacancy on the Fed board created when Adriana Kugler resigned in August. Her term ends in January 2026. If he's nominated and confirmed for a longer term, he'll resign from the council, he said.

Senator Jack Reed (D-RI) countered that if Miran serves on the Fed while on leave from the CEA, he'll be a White House employee who would also be serving on the Fed.

Developing... check back for updates.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Nasdaq tightens rules on firms hoarding tokens, sending crypto treasury stocks lower
data.description:
Shares of crypto treasury companies retreated on Thursday after a media report that Nasdaq (NASDAQ:NDAQ) was tightening oversight of listed firms seeking to boost valuations by issuing stock to buy tokens.

Strategy (NASDAQ:MSTR), a pioneer in the crypto treasury strategy, dipped 2.7%, ether-focused SharpLink Gaming (NASDAQ:SBET) gapped down 8.3%, Bitmine Immersion Technologies (NYSE:BMNR) -6.6%, Metaplanet (OTC:MTPLF) -6.7%, Mercury Fintech Holding (NASDAQ:MFH) -19% and Kindly MD (NASDAQ:NAKA) -5.2%, as of 11:22 a.m. ET.

Underlying crypto prices also saw selling pressure, with bitcoin (BTC-USD) down 2.1% in the past 24 hours to $109.3K and ether (ETH-USD) off 3.3% to $4.30K at press time.

Crypto treasury stocks have gained popularity as investors generally see them as an easy, leveraged way to get exposure to crypto through traditional markets. These vehicles have thrived under U.S. President Donald Trump's push to make the U.S. a leader in crypto with greater regulatory clarity and looser rules.

But the Nasdaq (NASDAQ:NDAQ) scrutiny complicates things. The stock exchange is telling certain companies they can't sell new shares to fund crypto purchases without first getting the green light from shareholders, The Information reported, citing people familiar with the matter

The move is meant to curb the corporate rush into so-called HODLing and ensure investors are fully informed before a company makes the crypto treasury pivot, the article said.

data.title: Amazon perks up as Anthropic partnership drives data center buildout, Trainium ramp
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) shares perked up 3% during Thursday trading as its relationship with the artificial intelligence startup Anthropic is helping to drive its data center buildout and the upcoming ramp of its in-house Trainium chip.

SemiAnalysis reported Amazon Web Services is currently building three data center campuses for Anthropic, which will total 1.3 gigawatts of capacity. These centers are expected to primarily utilize Amazon's Trainium chips. Amazon's data centers will hold the largest cluster of non-Nvidia (NVDA) AI chips, with nearly 1M Trainium2 chips in its biggest campus. 

Although the chips do not possess the power of Nvidia's most powerful processors, Anthropic was involved in the design process, meaning these chips deliver specifically what Anthropic needs to run its large language models, the report said.

Barclays analyst Ross Sandler also identified the potential for an acceleration in Amazon's revenue in the quarters ahead due in part to contributions from Anthropic.

Anthropic's primary revenue driver stems from high-value enterprise deals, which contrasts with competitor OpenAI's individual consumer approach through ChatGPT subscriptions.

data.title: RFK Jr. seeks to replace CDC vaccine panel members again - report
data.description:
• HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is believed to be seeking to replace -- for the second time -- members of a key CDC panel that makes recommendations on vaccines.
  • The potential move comes just two weeks before a key meeting of the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices is set to meet on Sept. 18 and 19.
  • Inside Medicine, citing CDC insiders, reported that seven new members of the panel have been selected, though they have yet to be formally appointed.
  • The outlet noted that several of the potential new members have spoken. out against mRNA vaccines and for ivermectin as a COVID-19 treatment. 
  • In June, RFK Jr. fired all the existing members of the ACIP and replaced them with his own vetted choices. 
  • HHS has yet to return a request to comment from Seeking Alpha. 
  • Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: DigitalOcean launches Single Sign-On to streamline cloud security and access
data.description:
• DigitalOcean Holdings (NYSE:DOCN) on Thursday said that it has launched Single Sign-On (SSO) support to provide customers with centralized, secure access to its cloud platform.
  • Built on the OpenID Connect (OIDC) protocol, the feature integrates with identity providers starting with Okta, and supports automated user provisioning, role-based access, and real-time deprovisioning.
  • The company said SSO will be available to all customers at no additional cost, aiming to simplify onboarding and access management for cloud-native teams.
  • “The addition of Single Sign-On underscores DigitalOcean’s commitment to delivering enterprise-grade security without enterprise complexity or cost,” DigitalOcean Chief Product and Technology Officer, Bratin Saha said in a statement.
  • DOCN is -2.37% to $31.28.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Paramount Skydance near deal for Bari Weiss's The Free Press - report
data.description:
Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY) is close to a deal to acquire The Free Press, the news and opinion media firm started by Bari Weiss.

David Ellison's Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY) is likely to pay between $100 million and $200 million for the company, which operates on Substack, according to a Puck report on Wednesday.

Ellison has been courting Weiss for almost a year, and formally pitched her on acquiring the firm at the Allen & Co. conference in July, according to the report. Those discussions have progressed in recent days, and the two sides have agreed in principle to a deal and lawyers are working on finalizing a formal agreement.

As part of the deal, Weiss is expected to be given a role at CBS News, that would task her with guiding the editorial direction of the division, Puck also reported.

data.title: Porsche to be removed from Germany's blue chip DAX
data.description:
Just three years after its initial public offering, Porsche (OTCPK:POAHY) (OTCPK:POAHF) is being dropped by Germany’s blue-chip index as a result of its falling stock price.

Plagued by a 28% sales decline in China -- the company’s third-largest market -- slow adoption of electrification, and more recently, U.S. trade tariffs that have dented the company’s profits, shares of the iconic German car company have dropped nearly 40% since its September 2022 IPO while the DAX has appreciated by 82% during the same time period.

In its most recent quarterly report, Porsche (OTCPK:POAHY) (OTCPK:POAHF) said U.S. tariffs cost the company $462M in the first half of the year. As the company chose to absorb these costs rather than pass them along to customers, Porsche (OTCPK:POAHY) (OTCPK:POAHF) subsequently lowered its sales and profit guidance for the year.

 “Our business model, which served us well for many decades, no longer works in its current form,” Porsche CEO Oliver Blume said in a memo to employees in July. At the time, the company planned additional cost cutting and would negotiate with its labor unions for additional wage reductions.

On September 22, Porsche (OTCPK:POAHY) (OTCPK:POAHF) will no longer trade on Germany’s DAX, moving to the midcap MDAX index, and will be replaced by German online marketplace Scout24 SE.

data.title: Blink Charging to integrate crypto payment options across its network
data.description:
Electric vehicle charging equipment and services provider Blink Charging (NASDAQ:BLNK) said on Thursday that it will integrate cryptocurrency payment options across its network by 2025 end.

The company said it plans to continue developing its crypto strategy, with additional details to be shared later this year.

data.title: RFK Jr. denies former CDC head was forced to pre-approve vaccine recommendations
data.description:
Testifying before the Senate Finance Committee on Thursday, Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. denied former CDC director Susan Monarez was pressured to preapprove recommendations of a vaccine advisory panel appointed by him in June.

His comments came after Monarez, who was fired last week, said in an Op-Ed column in The Wall Street Journal on Thursday that during a meeting she had with Kennedy days before her removal, she was pressured to rubber-stamp the recommendations.

“I didn’t say that to her,” RFK Jr. said, adding that “I never had a private meeting with her.”

Separately, earlier in the hearing, Kennedy said that under the Trump administration, the FDA is on track to approve the highest number of drugs this year. “At the FDA, we are on track to approve more drugs this year than any other time in history,” Kennedy said before the panel chaired by Sen. Mike Crapo (R-Idaho).

The hearing was scheduled to focus on President Donald Trump’s 2026 health care agenda and Kennedy's goal to Make America Healthy Again. It comes amid intense public scrutiny on the CDC and U.S. immunization policy under the leadership of RFK Jr., a well-known vaccine critic.

In June, the Kennedy scion removed all 17 members of the CDC’s Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices and named eight new members, some of whom were widely regarded as vaccine skeptics.

Under RFK Jr., the Trump administration fired former CDC director Susan Monarez only 29 days into her job last week, reportedly over her refusal to accept the recommendations of the newly formed ACIP panel.

Leading vaccine makers: GSK (NYSE:GSK), Sanofi (NASDAQ:SNY), Pfizer (NYSE:PFE), BioNTech (BNTX), Moderna (MRNA), and Novavax (NVAX).

data.title: GitLab sinks on second-half slowdown, but analysts remain positive on long-term setup
data.description:
GitLab (NASDAQ:GTLB) shares sank 8% during early market action on Thursday following second quarter fiscal 2026 results that demonstrated a possible slowdown in the second half of the year.

The software development platform is also undergoing some turnover at the executive levels. Still, most financial firms remain positive on the company and its overall outlook.

For example, RBC Capital Markets reiterated its Outperform rating and $58 price target.

"Gitlab reported solid Q2 results with revenue growth accelerating to +29% y/y vs. +27% last qtr reflecting a 4.0% revenue beat vs. 0.7% last qtr," said RBC analysts, led by Matthew Hedberg, in a Thursday note.

"We feel business trends remains durable and de-risked 2H estimates create a good setup," he added.

KeyBanc retained its Overweight rating but lowered its price target to $53 from $60.

"GitLab reported a solid F2Q beat, driven in part by one-time benefits from linearity and mix shift toward self-managed, but left the full-year revenue guide unchanged to account for GTM/leadership changes, which implies a notable deceleration in the 2H," said KeyBanc analysts, led by Jason Celino, in a note.

"While we remain encouraged by GitLab's consolidation opportunities, we lower our PT to $53 based on 7x FY27 EV/R to acknowledge the potential NT overhang from macro, GTM evolution, and AI disruptions," he added.

Meanwhile, Bank of America reiterated its Buy rating and stout $72 price target.

BofA believes GitLab likely did not increase its fiscal 2026 revenue guidance due to conservatism and the recent turnover at the top.

"We believe it's just conservatism," said BofA analysts, led by Koji Ikeda, in a note. "Especially considering there are some executive changes happening too. Demand strength can be seen in F2Q26 subscription revenue, where growth accelerated to +30.3% y/y, from +28.6% last quarter. We continue to believe GitLab is a long-term share gainer in the DevSecOps category."

GitLab announced its chief financial officer, Brian Robins, who has served in the position since 2020, is stepping down to take the same position at Snowflake (SNOW) on September 22. GitLab must now search for a new CFO right after it appointed two new executives. On September 2, the DevSecOps platform revealed the appointments of Manav Khurana as chief product and marketing officer and Manu Narayan as chief information officer.

Before joining GitLab, Khurana served as the chief product officer at New Relic and has also held product and marketing leadership roles at Twilio (TWLO), Aruba HPE (HPE), and Motorola (MSI). Prior to joining GitLab, Narayan served as the vice president of business technology at Confluent (CFLT).

Barclays reiterated its Equal weight rating but slightly lowered its price target to $44 from $47.

"We are encouraged that GTLB is making a more concentrated effort to improve new business growth, which has been a key question for its long-term growth algorithm," said Barclays analysts Raimo Lenschow and Eamon Coughlin, in a note. "However, it will be executing these initiatives while looking for a new CFO and with a new CRO and a new CMO/CPO."

Other companies in the DevOps space were down on Thursday as well, with Atlassian (TEAM) falling 1.7% and JFrog (FROG) inching down by 0.5%.
